  const result: AuditResult = {
    file: "/path/to/SKILL.md",
    findings: [],
    score: 100,
    passed: true,
  };

Members:

#### file

string

Path to the scanned file.

#### findings

AuditFinding[]

All findings for this file.

#### score

number

Security score from 0 (dangerous) to 100 (clean).

#### passed

boolean

Whether the file passed the audit (no critical/high findings).

### InjectionCheckResult

InjectionCheckResult

Result of checking a single instruction file for CAAMP injection status.

Example:
  const check: InjectionCheckResult = {
    file: "/project/CLAUDE.md",
    provider: "claude-code",
    status: "current",
    fileExists: true,
  };

Members:

#### file

string

Absolute path to the instruction file.

#### provider

string

Provider ID that owns this instruction file.

#### status

InjectionStatus

Current injection status.

#### fileExists

boolean

Whether the instruction file exists on disk.

### McpServerEntry

McpServerEntry

An MCP server entry read from a provider's config file.

Example:
  const entry: McpServerEntry = {
    name: "filesystem",
    providerId: "claude-code",
    providerName: "Claude Code",
    scope: "project",
    configPath: "/project/.claude.json",
    config: { command: "npx", args: ["-y", "@mcp/server-filesystem"] },
  };

Members:

#### name

string

Server name (the key in the config file).

#### providerId

string

Provider ID that owns this config file.

#### providerName

string

Human-readable provider name.

#### scope

"project" | "global"

Whether from project or global config.

#### configPath

string

Absolute path to the config file.

#### config

Record<string, unknown>

Raw server configuration object.

### GlobalOptions

GlobalOptions

Global CLI options shared across all CAAMP commands.

Example:
  const opts: GlobalOptions = {
    agent: ["claude-code", "cursor"],
    global: true,
    json: true,
  };

Members:

#### agent

string[] | undefined

Target agent IDs (repeatable).

#### global

boolean | undefined

Operate on global config instead of project.

#### yes

boolean | undefined

Skip confirmation prompts.

#### all

boolean | undefined

Target all detected agents.

#### json

boolean | undefined

Output as JSON.

#### dryRun

boolean | undefined

Preview changes without writing.

#### verbose

boolean | undefined

Enable debug logging.

#### quiet

boolean | undefined

Suppress non-error output.

### PlatformPaths

PlatformPaths

OS-appropriate directory paths for CAAMP's global storage.

Members:

#### data

string

User data dir. Override with AGENTS_HOME env var.

#### config

string

OS config dir (XDG_CONFIG_HOME / Library/Preferences / %APPDATA%).

#### cache

string

OS cache dir.

#### log

string

OS log dir.

#### temp

string

OS temp dir.

### SystemInfo

SystemInfo

Snapshot of the current system environment and resolved platform paths.

Members:

#### platform

NodeJS.Platform

Operating system platform identifier.

#### arch

string

CPU architecture (e.g. `"x64"`, `"arm64"`).

#### release

string

OS kernel release version string.

#### hostname

string

Machine hostname.

#### nodeVersion

string

Node.js version string (e.g. `"v20.11.0"`).

#### paths

PlatformPaths

Resolved platform directory paths.

### PlatformLocations

PlatformLocations

Platform-specific directory locations for agent configuration.

Members:

#### home

string

The user's home directory path.

#### config

string

The platform-specific configuration directory.

#### vscodeConfig

string

The VS Code user settings directory.

#### zedConfig

string

The Zed editor configuration directory.

#### claudeDesktopConfig

string

The Claude Desktop application configuration directory.

#### applications

string[]

List of application directories (macOS only).

### InjectionTemplate

InjectionTemplate

Structured template for injection content.

Members:

#### references

string[]

References to include (e.g. `"\@AGENTS.md"`, `"\@.cleo/project-context.json"`).

#### content

string[] | undefined

Inline content blocks (raw markdown/text).

### EnsureProviderInstructionFileOptions

EnsureProviderInstructionFileOptions

Options for ensuring a provider instruction file.

Members:

#### references

string[]

`\@` references to inject (e.g. `["\@AGENTS.md"]`).

#### content

string[] | undefined

Optional inline content blocks.

#### scope

"project" | "global" | undefined

Whether this is a global or project-level file.

### EnsureProviderInstructionFileResult

EnsureProviderInstructionFileResult

Result of ensuring a provider instruction file.

Members:

#### filePath

string

Absolute path to the instruction file.

#### instructFile

string

Instruction file name from the provider registry.

#### action

"created" | "added" | "consolidated" | "updated" | "intact"

Action taken.

#### providerId

string

Provider ID.

### DetectionResult

DetectionResult

Result of detecting whether a provider is installed on the system.

Example:
  const provider = getProvider("claude-code")!;
  const result = detectProvider(provider);
  if (result.installed) {
    console.log(`Found via: ${result.methods.join(", ")}`);
  }

Members:

#### provider

Provider

The provider that was checked.

#### installed

boolean

Whether the provider was detected as installed.

#### methods

string[]

Detection methods that matched (e.g. `["binary", "directory"]`).

#### projectDetected

boolean

Whether the provider has project-level config in the current directory.

### DetectionCacheOptions

DetectionCacheOptions

Options for controlling the detection result cache.

Members:

#### forceRefresh

boolean | undefined

Whether to bypass the cache and force a fresh detection scan.

#### ttlMs

number | undefined

Time-to-live for cached results in milliseconds.

### SkillInstallResult

SkillInstallResult

Result of installing a skill to the canonical location and linking to agents.

Example:
  const result = await installSkill(sourcePath, "my-skill", providers, true);
  if (result.success) {
    console.log(`Installed to ${result.canonicalPath}`);
    console.log(`Linked to: ${result.linkedAgents.join(", ")}`);
  }

Members:

#### name

string

Skill name.

#### canonicalPath

string

Absolute path to the canonical installation directory.

#### linkedAgents

string[]

Provider IDs that were successfully linked.

#### errors

string[]

Error messages from failed link operations.

#### success

boolean

Whether at least one agent was successfully linked.

### SkillBatchOperation

SkillBatchOperation

Single skill operation entry used by batch orchestration.

Members:

#### sourcePath

string

The filesystem path to the skill source files.

#### skillName

string

The unique name for the skill being installed.

#### isGlobal

boolean | undefined

Whether to install globally or project-scoped, defaults to true.

### BatchInstallOptions

BatchInstallOptions

Options for rollback-capable batch installation.

Members:

#### providers

Provider[] | undefined

Explicit list of providers to target, auto-detected if omitted.

#### minimumPriority

ProviderPriority | undefined

Minimum provider priority threshold for filtering.

#### skills

SkillBatchOperation[] | undefined

Skill operations to apply in the batch.

#### projectDir

string | undefined

Project root directory, defaults to `process.cwd()`.

### BatchInstallResult

BatchInstallResult

Result of rollback-capable batch installation.

Members:

#### success

boolean

Whether all operations completed successfully.

#### providerIds

string[]

IDs of providers that were targeted.

#### skillsApplied

number

Number of skill installations that were applied.

#### rollbackPerformed

boolean

Whether rollback was performed due to a failure.

#### rollbackErrors

string[]

Error messages from any failures during rollback.

#### error

string | undefined

Error message from the operation that triggered rollback.

### InstructionUpdateSummary

InstructionUpdateSummary

Result of a single-operation instruction update across providers.

Members:

#### scope

Scope

The scope at which instructions were updated.

#### updatedFiles

number

The total number of instruction files that were modified.

#### actions

{ file: string; action: "created" | "added" | "consolidated" | "updated" | "intact"; providers: string[]; configFormats: ConfigFormat[]; }[]

Detailed action log per instruction file.

### ResolveTierDirOptions

ResolveTierDirOptions

Options accepted by `resolveTierDir`.

Members:

#### tier

HarnessTier

Tier to resolve.

#### kind

HarnessAssetKind

Asset kind (extensions, prompts, themes, sessions, cant).

#### projectDir

string | undefined

Project directory used when `tier` is `project`. Ignored for other tiers. When omitted with `tier='project'` the caller MUST substitute `process.cwd()` before invoking the resolver, so the harness never silently resolves to an unexpected working directory.

### ResolveDefaultTargetProvidersOptions

ResolveDefaultTargetProvidersOptions

Options accepted by `resolveDefaultTargetProviders` (defined in `./index.ts`).

Members:

#### explicit

Provider[] | undefined

Explicit list of providers requested by the user (e.g. via `--agent`).

### ExtensionEntry

ExtensionEntry

Metadata describing a Pi extension discovered on disk.

Members:

#### name

string

Extension name (file basename without the `.ts` extension).

#### tier

HarnessTier

Tier at which this entry lives.

#### path

string

Absolute on-disk path to the extension file.

#### shadowed

boolean | undefined

When `true`, this entry is shadowed by a higher-precedence entry with the same name. Exposed so list output can warn about cross-tier name collisions per ADR-035 §D1.

### PromptEntry

PromptEntry

Metadata describing a Pi prompt directory discovered on disk.

Members:

#### name

string

Prompt name (directory basename).

#### tier

HarnessTier

Tier at which this entry lives.

#### path

string

Absolute on-disk path to the prompt directory.

#### shadowed

boolean | undefined

See `ExtensionEntry.shadowed`.

### ThemeEntry

ThemeEntry

Metadata describing a Pi theme discovered on disk.

Members:

#### name

string

Theme name (file basename without the extension).

#### tier

HarnessTier

Tier at which this entry lives.

#### path

string

Absolute on-disk path to the theme file.

#### fileExt

string

File extension of the theme file (e.g. `".ts"`, `".json"`).

#### shadowed

boolean | undefined

See `ExtensionEntry.shadowed`.

### HarnessInstallOptions

HarnessInstallOptions

Options accepted by the Pi install verbs (extensions, prompts, themes).

Members:

#### force

boolean | undefined

When `true`, overwrite an existing file at the target tier. When `false` (the default) the install verb throws if the target exists.

### CantProfileCounts

CantProfileCounts

Counts of top-level CANT sections discovered in a `.cant` file.

Members:

#### agentCount

number

Number of `agent { ... }` sections in the document.

#### workflowCount

number

Number of `workflow { ... }` sections in the document.

#### pipelineCount

number

Number of `pipeline { ... }` sections in the document.

#### hookCount

number

Number of hook bodies discovered. Top-level `Hook` sections plus the `hooks` array nested inside every Agent section are summed.

#### skillCount

number

Number of distinct skill names referenced via an Agent section's `skills:` property (e.g. `skills: ["ct-cleo", "ct-task-executor"]`). The count is de-duplicated across agents within a single document.

### CantProfileEntry

CantProfileEntry

Metadata describing a `.cant` profile installed at one of the three Pi harness tiers.

Members:

#### name

string

Profile name (basename of the `.cant` file without the extension).

#### tier

HarnessTier

Tier at which this entry lives.

#### sourcePath

string

Absolute on-disk path to the `.cant` file.

#### counts

CantProfileCounts

Parsed section counts for the profile.

#### shadowedByHigherTier

boolean | undefined

When `true`, this entry is shadowed by a higher-precedence entry with the same name. Mirrors the shadow flag emitted by `Harness.listExtensions` so the same UI logic applies.

### CantValidationDiagnostic

CantValidationDiagnostic

Diagnostic emitted by the cant-core 42-rule validator, normalised to the harness layer's vocabulary.

Members:

#### ruleId

string

Cant-core rule id (`PARSE`, `S01`, `P06`, ...).

#### message

string

Human-readable diagnostic message.

#### line

number

1-based line number where the diagnostic was emitted.

#### col

number

1-based column number where the diagnostic was emitted.

#### severity

"error" | "info" | "warning" | "hint"

Severity bucket from cant-core (`error`, `warning`, `info`, `hint`).

### ValidateCantProfileResult

ValidateCantProfileResult

Result of validating a `.cant` profile via the cant-core 42-rule engine.

Members:

#### valid

boolean

Whether validation passed (no error-severity diagnostics).

#### errors

CantValidationDiagnostic[]

All diagnostics emitted by the 42-rule engine, in source order.

#### counts

CantProfileCounts

Section counts for the profile (zero when parsing failed).

### SessionSummary

SessionSummary

Summary header extracted from the first line of a Pi session JSONL file.

Members:

#### id

string

Session identifier as recorded in the line-1 header.

#### version

number

Session version as recorded in the line-1 header (e.g. `3`).

#### timestamp

string | null

ISO-8601 timestamp from the line-1 header, or `null` when absent.

#### cwd

string | null

Working directory recorded when the session was created.

#### parentSession

string | null

Parent session id, if this session was forked from another.

#### filePath

string

Absolute path to the session JSONL file on disk.

#### mtimeMs

number

File modification time in milliseconds since the epoch.

### SessionDocument

SessionDocument

Raw content of a Pi session JSONL file, preserved line-by-line.

Members:

#### summary

SessionSummary

Header summary (same shape as `SessionSummary`).

#### entries

string[]

Raw JSONL lines in file order, excluding the line-1 header.

### PiModelDefinition

PiModelDefinition

Pi model definition as recorded under `models.json:providers[].models`.

Members:

#### id

string

Model id within the provider (e.g. `"claude-opus-4-20250514"`).

#### name

string

Human-readable model name.

#### reasoning

boolean | undefined

Whether the model supports reasoning/thinking tokens.

#### input

("text" | "image")[] | undefined

Allowed input modalities (e.g. `["text"]`, `["text", "image"]`).

#### contextWindow

number | undefined

Context window size in tokens.

#### maxTokens

number | undefined

Maximum output tokens.

### PiModelProvider

PiModelProvider

Pi provider block as recorded under `models.json:providers[id]`.

Members:

#### baseUrl

string | undefined

Custom base URL for the provider (overrides default).

#### apiKey

string | undefined

API key or `$ENV_VAR` reference.

#### models

PiModelDefinition[] | undefined

Custom model definitions declared by the user.

### PiModelsConfig

PiModelsConfig

Entire `models.json` document shape used by Pi.

Members:

#### providers

Record<string, PiModelProvider>

Map of provider id → provider block.

### ModelListEntry

ModelListEntry

A model entry as surfaced by `Harness.listModels`.

Members:

#### provider

string

Provider id (e.g. `"anthropic"`).

#### id

string

Model id within the provider.

#### name

string | null

Human-readable name, from `models.json` when available.

#### enabled

boolean

`true` when the model is present in `settings.json:enabledModels`.

#### isDefault

boolean

`true` when the model is the configured default.

#### custom

boolean

`true` when this model is defined in `models.json` (custom). When `false`, the entry originates from `settings.json:enabledModels` only and is assumed to resolve against Pi's built-in registry.

### SubagentTask

SubagentTask

Description of a subagent task to be spawned under a harness.

Members:

#### targetProviderId

string

Provider id of the agent to spawn (e.g. `"claude-code"`).

#### prompt

string

The prompt / instruction to give the spawned agent.

#### taskId

string | undefined

Stable task identifier used to derive the child session filename and to correlate streamed events with their originating task.

#### parentSessionId

string | undefined

Identifier of the parent session that owns this subagent.

#### parentSessionPath

string | undefined

Absolute path to the parent session JSONL file.

#### cwd

string | undefined

Working directory for the spawned agent.

#### env

Record<string, string> | undefined

Environment variable overrides layered atop the parent environment.

#### signal

AbortSignal | undefined

Abort signal. When it aborts, the harness will terminate the subagent via the configured SIGTERM-then-SIGKILL cleanup sequence.

### SubagentSpawnOptions

SubagentSpawnOptions

Per-call options that override harness-wide spawn defaults.

Members:

#### onStream

((event: SubagentStreamEvent) => void) | undefined

Streaming callback invoked once per parsed event from the child.

#### terminateGraceMs

number | undefined

Override the SIGTERM grace window before SIGKILL fires.

#### env

Record<string, string> | undefined

Environment variable overrides layered atop the task-level env.

#### cwd

string | undefined

Working directory override that wins over `SubagentTask.cwd`.

### SubagentStreamEvent

SubagentStreamEvent

One streaming event surfaced through `SubagentSpawnOptions.onStream`.

Members:

#### kind

"message" | "stderr" | "exit" | "link"

Event kind discriminator.

#### subagentId

string

Subagent identifier (matches `SubagentHandle.subagentId`).

#### lineNumber

number | undefined

1-based line number within the child's stdout stream. Only set for `"message"` events that originated from a parsed stdout line.

#### payload

unknown

Event payload, shaped according to `kind`.

### SubagentExitResult

SubagentExitResult

Resolution value of `SubagentHandle.exitPromise`.

Members:

#### code

number | null

Process exit code, or `null` when the child was terminated by a signal before exiting normally.

#### signal

NodeJS.Signals | null

Terminating signal, or `null` when the child exited normally.

#### childSessionPath

string

Absolute path to the child session JSONL file on disk.

#### durationMs

number

Wall-clock duration from spawn to exit, in milliseconds.

### SubagentLinkEntry

SubagentLinkEntry

`subagent_link` custom entry written into the parent session JSONL.

Members:

#### type

"subagent_link"

Entry type discriminator (always `"subagent_link"`).

#### subagentId

string

Subagent identifier matching `SubagentHandle.subagentId`.

#### taskId

string

Task identifier from `SubagentTask.taskId`.

#### childSessionPath

string

Absolute path to the child session JSONL file.

#### startedAt

string

ISO-8601 timestamp captured when the child was spawned.

### SubagentResult

SubagentResult

Final result of a subagent's execution (legacy v1 shape).

Members:

#### exitCode

number | null

Process exit code, or `null` if the process was killed by a signal.

#### stdout

string

Full stdout captured from the subagent.

#### stderr

string

Full stderr captured from the subagent.

#### parsed

unknown

Parsed JSON output, when the target supports a JSON output mode and emitted well-formed JSON on stdout.

### SubagentHandle

SubagentHandle

Live handle to a running subagent.

Members:

#### subagentId

string

Stable subagent identifier generated at spawn time.

#### taskId

string

Task identifier from `SubagentTask.taskId` (or generated default).

#### childSessionPath

string

Absolute path to the child session JSONL file on disk.

#### pid

number | null

PID of the spawned process, or `null` if spawning did not yield one.

#### startedAt

Date

Wall-clock timestamp captured immediately after spawn.

#### exitPromise

Promise<SubagentExitResult>

Promise resolving to the rich exit result once the child process has fully terminated. NEVER rejects — failures are encoded in the resolved value (non-zero code, non-null signal, partial output in the session file).

#### result

Promise<SubagentResult>

Promise resolving to the legacy `SubagentResult` shape.

#### terminate()

() => Promise<void>

Terminate the subagent gracefully.

#### abort

() => void

Synchronously trigger the cleanup sequence (legacy v1 alias for `terminate`).

#### recentStderr()

() => string[]

Snapshot of the most recent stderr lines captured for this child.

### Harness

Harness

Contract every first-class harness must implement.

Members:

#### id

string

Short id matching the provider id (e.g. `"pi"`).

#### provider

Provider

The underlying resolved provider entry.

#### installSkill()

(sourcePath: string, skillName: string, scope: HarnessScope) => Promise<void>

Install a skill using the harness's native mechanism.

Parameters:
- sourcePath: Absolute path to the skill directory to install.
- skillName: Target skill name (becomes the directory name on disk).
- scope: Install target scope.

#### removeSkill()

(skillName: string, scope: HarnessScope) => Promise<void>

Remove a skill previously installed via this harness.

Parameters:
- skillName: Skill name to remove.
- scope: Install target scope.

#### listSkills()

(scope: HarnessScope) => Promise<string[]>

List skills installed in this harness's skill directory for a scope.

Parameters:
- scope: Scope to inspect.

Returns: Array of skill directory names.

#### injectInstructions()

(content: string, scope: HarnessScope) => Promise<void>

Inject content into the harness's instruction file using a marker-based idempotent block.

Parameters:
- content: The content to inject inside the marker block.
- scope: Instruction file scope.

#### removeInstructions()

(scope: HarnessScope) => Promise<void>

Remove the CAAMP injection block from the harness's instruction file.

Parameters:
- scope: Instruction file scope.

#### spawnSubagent()

((task: SubagentTask, opts?: SubagentSpawnOptions) => Promise<SubagentHandle>) | undefined

Spawn a subagent under this harness's control.

Parameters:
- task: Subagent task specification.
- opts: Per-call streaming and cleanup overrides.

Returns: A live subagent handle.

#### configureModels()

((modelPatterns: string[], scope: HarnessScope) => Promise<void>) | undefined

Configure which models are available in the harness's model picker.

Parameters:
- modelPatterns: Glob patterns enumerating enabled models.
- scope: Settings scope.

#### readSettings()

(scope: HarnessScope) => Promise<unknown>

Read the harness's current settings as an opaque object.

Parameters:
- scope: Settings scope.

Returns: Opaque settings blob.

#### writeSettings()

(patch: Record<string, unknown>, scope: HarnessScope) => Promise<void>

Deep-merge a patch into the harness's settings and persist the result.

Parameters:
- patch: Partial settings object to merge.
- scope: Settings scope.

#### installExtension()

((sourcePath: string, name: string, tier: HarnessTier, projectDir?: string, opts?: HarnessInstallOptions) => Promise<{ targetPath: string; tier: HarnessTier; }>) | undefined

Install a Pi extension TypeScript file from a local source path into the given tier.

Parameters:
- sourcePath: Absolute path to the source `.ts` file on disk.
- name: Extension name (used as the target file basename).
- tier: Target tier (`project`/`user`/`global`).
- projectDir: Project directory (required when `tier='project'`).
- opts: Install options (see `HarnessInstallOptions`).

#### removeExtension()

((name: string, tier: HarnessTier, projectDir?: string) => Promise<boolean>) | undefined

Remove a Pi extension by name from the given tier.

Parameters:
- name: Extension name (basename without `.ts`).
- tier: Target tier to remove from.
- projectDir: Project directory (required when `tier='project'`).

Returns: `true` when a file was removed, `false` when none existed.

#### listExtensions()

((projectDir?: string) => Promise<ExtensionEntry[]>) | undefined

List Pi extensions across all tiers, precedence-ordered.

Parameters:
- projectDir: Project directory for the `project` tier. When   omitted the `project` tier is skipped rather than failing.

#### listSessions()

((opts?: { includeSubagents?: boolean; }) => Promise<SessionSummary[]>) | undefined

List Pi sessions from the user-tier sessions directory.

Parameters:
- opts: Options controlling which directories to scan.

#### showSession()

((id: string) => Promise<SessionDocument>) | undefined

Load a Pi session's full body by id.

Parameters:
- id: Session id as recorded in the line-1 header.

#### listModels()

((scope: HarnessScope) => Promise<ModelListEntry[]>) | undefined

List every model known to Pi — both custom (`models.json`) and enabled selections (`settings.json:enabledModels`).

Parameters:
- scope: Legacy two-tier scope (global/project) that   determines which `models.json` and `settings.json` files to read.

#### readModelsConfig()

((scope: HarnessScope) => Promise<PiModelsConfig>) | undefined

Read `models.json` for the given scope.

#### writeModelsConfig()

((config: PiModelsConfig, scope: HarnessScope) => Promise<void>) | undefined

Write `models.json` for the given scope atomically.

#### installPrompt()

((sourceDir: string, name: string, tier: HarnessTier, projectDir?: string, opts?: HarnessInstallOptions) => Promise<{ targetPath: string; tier: HarnessTier; }>) | undefined

Install a Pi prompt from a source directory into the given tier.

#### listPrompts()

((projectDir?: string) => Promise<PromptEntry[]>) | undefined

List Pi prompts across all tiers.

#### removePrompt()

((name: string, tier: HarnessTier, projectDir?: string) => Promise<boolean>) | undefined

Remove a Pi prompt by name from the given tier.

#### installTheme()

((sourceFile: string, name: string, tier: HarnessTier, projectDir?: string, opts?: HarnessInstallOptions) => Promise<{ targetPath: string; tier: HarnessTier; }>) | undefined

Install a Pi theme from a source file into the given tier.

#### listThemes()

((projectDir?: string) => Promise<ThemeEntry[]>) | undefined

List Pi themes across all tiers.

#### removeTheme()

((name: string, tier: HarnessTier, projectDir?: string) => Promise<boolean>) | undefined

Remove a Pi theme by name from the given tier.

#### installCantProfile()

((sourcePath: string, name: string, tier: HarnessTier, projectDir?: string, opts?: HarnessInstallOptions) => Promise<{ targetPath: string; tier: HarnessTier; counts: CantProfileCounts; }>) | undefined

Install a CANT profile (`.cant` file) into the given tier.

Parameters:
- sourcePath: Absolute path to the source `.cant` file on disk.
- name: Profile name (used as the target file basename).
- tier: Target tier (`project`/`user`/`global`).
- projectDir: Project directory (required when `tier='project'`).
- opts: Install options (see `HarnessInstallOptions`).

#### removeCantProfile()

((name: string, tier: HarnessTier, projectDir?: string) => Promise<boolean>) | undefined

Remove a CANT profile by name from the given tier.

Parameters:
- name: Profile name (basename without `.cant`).
- tier: Target tier to remove from.
- projectDir: Project directory (required when `tier='project'`).

Returns: `true` when a file was removed, `false` when none existed.

#### listCantProfiles()

((projectDir?: string) => Promise<CantProfileEntry[]>) | undefined

List CANT profiles across all tiers, precedence-ordered.

Parameters:
- projectDir: Project directory for the `project` tier. When   omitted the `project` tier is skipped rather than failing.

#### validateCantProfile()

((sourcePath: string) => Promise<ValidateCantProfileResult>) | undefined

Validate a `.cant` file via cant-core's 42-rule engine without installing anything.

Parameters:
- sourcePath: Absolute path to the `.cant` file on disk.

### LAFSEnvelope

LAFSEnvelope<T>

Generic LAFS Envelope structure for type-safe command results.

Members:

#### $schema

"https://lafs.dev/schemas/v1/envelope.schema.json"

JSON Schema URI for envelope validation.

#### _meta

LAFSMeta

Envelope metadata (timestamps, request IDs, MVI level).

#### success

boolean

Whether the operation succeeded.

#### result

T | null

Operation result payload, or `null` on error.

#### error

LAFSError | null

Error details, or `null` on success.

#### page

LAFSPage | null

Pagination metadata, or `null` when not applicable.

### FormatOptions

FormatOptions

Format resolution options.

Members:

#### jsonFlag

boolean | undefined

Whether `--json` was explicitly passed.

#### humanFlag

boolean | undefined

Whether `--human` was explicitly passed.

#### projectDefault

"json" | "human" | undefined

Project-level default format when no flag is given.

### LAFSCommandOptions

LAFSCommandOptions

Standard command options interface for LAFS-compliant commands.

Members:

#### json

boolean | undefined

Whether to force JSON output.

#### human

boolean | undefined

Whether to force human-readable output.

### ProviderTargetOptions

ProviderTargetOptions

Options for resolving which providers to target in advanced commands.

Members:

#### all

boolean | undefined

When true, target all registry providers including undetected ones.

#### agent

string[] | undefined

Specific provider IDs or aliases to target.

### McpDetectionEntry

McpDetectionEntry

Result of a single provider's MCP installation probe.

Members:

#### providerId

string

Provider id (e.g. `"claude-code"`).

#### providerName

string

Human-readable provider name.

#### scope

McpScope

Resolved scope of the probed config file.

#### configPath

string

Absolute path to the provider's MCP config file.

#### exists

boolean

Whether the config file exists on disk.

#### serverCount

number | null

Number of server entries found, or `null` when the file is missing/unparseable.

#### lastModified

string | null

ISO 8601 timestamp of the file's last modification, or `null` when the file is missing.

### InstallMcpServerOptions

InstallMcpServerOptions

Options accepted by `installMcpServer`.

Members:

#### scope

McpScope

Scope to write to (project|global).

#### force

boolean | undefined

When `true`, overwrite an existing server entry instead of failing.

#### projectDir

string | undefined

Project directory used for the `project` scope.

### InstallMcpServerResult

InstallMcpServerResult

Result of an `installMcpServer` call.

Members:

#### installed

boolean

Whether the entry was written to the config file.

#### conflicted

boolean

Whether the target server name already existed before the call.

#### sourcePath

string

Absolute path to the config file that was (or would have been) written.

#### providerId

string

Provider id the entry was written for.

#### serverName

string

Server name that was written.

### RemoveMcpServerOptions

RemoveMcpServerOptions

Options accepted by `removeMcpServer` and `removeMcpServerFromAll`.

Members:

#### scope

McpScope

Scope to target (project|global).

#### projectDir

string | undefined

Project directory used for the `project` scope.

### RemoveMcpServerResult

RemoveMcpServerResult

Result of a single-provider `removeMcpServer` call.

Members:

#### providerId

string

Provider id the call targeted.

#### serverName

string

Server name the call targeted.

#### sourcePath

string | null

Resolved config file path, or `null` when the provider had no MCP capability.

#### removed

boolean

Whether an entry was actually deleted.

#### reason

"no-mcp-capability" | "no-config-path" | "file-missing" | "entry-missing" | null

Diagnostic discriminator when `removed` is `false`.  - `"no-mcp-capability"` — provider does not consume MCP servers - `"no-config-path"` — provider has no config path for the scope - `"file-missing"` — config file does not exist on disk - `"entry-missing"` — config file exists but had no matching entry  Set to `null` when `removed` is `true`.

### McpCommandBaseOptions

McpCommandBaseOptions

Standard option shape accepted by every `caamp mcp <verb>` command.

Members:

#### scope

string | undefined

`--scope project|global` (default: project).

#### projectDir

string | undefined

`--project-dir <path>` — override cwd for the `project` scope.

### McpDetectOptions

McpDetectOptions

Options accepted by `caamp mcp detect`.

Members:

#### onlyExisting

boolean | undefined

Show only providers that actually have a config file on disk.

### McpInstallOptions

McpInstallOptions

Options accepted by `caamp mcp install`.

Members:

#### provider

string | undefined

Provider id to install into (required).

#### from

string | undefined

Optional path to a JSON file containing an `McpServerConfig`.

#### env

string[] | undefined

Repeatable `KEY=VALUE` env assignments.

#### force

boolean | undefined

Overwrite an existing entry instead of failing.

### McpListOptions

McpListOptions

Options accepted by `caamp mcp list`.

Members:

#### provider

string | undefined

Restrict the listing to a single provider id.

### McpRemoveOptions

McpRemoveOptions

Options accepted by `caamp mcp remove`.

Members:

#### provider

string | undefined

Provider id to remove from (mutually exclusive with --all-providers).

#### allProviders

boolean | undefined

Remove from every MCP-capable provider in the registry.

### GitFetchResult

GitFetchResult

Result of fetching a Git repository to a local temporary directory.

Members:

#### localPath

string

Absolute path to the fetched content on disk.

#### cleanup

() => Promise<void>

Cleanup function that removes the temporary directory.

### PiCommandBaseOptions

PiCommandBaseOptions

Standard option shape accepted by every `caamp pi <verb>` command.

Members:

#### scope

string | undefined

`--scope project|user|global`.

#### force

boolean | undefined

`--force` — overwrite existing targets on install verbs.

#### projectDir

string | undefined

`--project-dir <path>` — override cwd for the `project` tier.

### PiCantListOptions

PiCantListOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi cant list`.

Members:

#### projectDir

string | undefined

Project directory used for the `project` tier.

### PiCantInstallOptions

PiCantInstallOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi cant install`.

Members:

#### name

string | undefined

Profile name override. Defaults to the inferred source name.

### PiExtensionsListOptions

PiExtensionsListOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi extensions list`.

Members:

#### projectDir

string | undefined

Project directory used for the `project` tier.

### PiExtensionsInstallOptions

PiExtensionsInstallOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi extensions install`.

Members:

#### name

string | undefined

Extension name override. Defaults to the inferred source name.

### PiModelsCommandOptions

PiModelsCommandOptions

Options accepted by every `caamp pi models` verb.

Members:

#### global

boolean | undefined

`--global` targets the Pi global state root instead of the project.

### PiModelsAddOptions

PiModelsAddOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi models add`.

Members:

#### displayName

string | undefined

Human-readable model name.

#### baseUrl

string | undefined

Override the provider base URL.

#### reasoning

boolean | undefined

Reasoning-capable flag.

#### contextWindow

string | undefined

Context window size in tokens.

#### maxTokens

string | undefined

Maximum output tokens.

### PiPromptsInstallOptions

PiPromptsInstallOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi prompts install`.

Members:

#### name

string | undefined

Override the inferred prompt directory name.

### PiSessionsListOptions

PiSessionsListOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi sessions list`.

Members:

#### includeSubagents

boolean | undefined

Include sessions under the `subagents/` subdirectory (default: true).

### PiSessionsExportOptions

PiSessionsExportOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi sessions export`.

Members:

#### jsonl

boolean | undefined

Emit raw JSONL. Mutually exclusive with `md`.

#### md

boolean | undefined

Emit Markdown (filtered to message/custom_message entries).

#### output

string | undefined

Write to this file path instead of stdout.

### PiThemesInstallOptions

PiThemesInstallOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi themes install`.

Members:

#### name

string | undefined

Override the inferred theme name.

### CanonicalEventDefinition

CanonicalEventDefinition

Definition of a canonical hook event including its category and behavior.

Members:

#### category

"agent" | "context" | "memory" | "pipeline" | "prompt" | "session" | "task" | "tool"

The lifecycle category this event belongs to (e.g. `"session"`, `"tool"`).

#### description

string

Human-readable description of when this event fires.

#### canBlock

boolean

Whether a hook handler can block or cancel the associated action.

### HookMapping

HookMapping

Mapping of a single canonical event to a provider's native representation.

Members:

#### nativeName

string | null

The provider-native event name, or `null` if the event has no native equivalent.

#### supported

boolean

Whether this canonical event is supported by the provider.

#### notes

string | undefined

Optional notes about support limitations or behavioral differences.

### ProviderHookProfile

ProviderHookProfile

Complete hook profile for a single provider.

Members:

#### hookSystem

HookSystemType

The type of hook system the provider uses (`"config"`, `"plugin"`, or `"none"`).

#### hookConfigPath

string | null

Filesystem path template to the provider's hook configuration file, or `null`.

#### hookFormat

string | null

The configuration format used for hooks (e.g. `"json"`, `"yaml"`), or `null`.

#### handlerTypes

HookHandlerType[]

The handler execution mechanisms this provider supports.

#### experimental

boolean

Whether the provider's hook system is considered experimental or unstable.

#### mappings

Record<"ConfigChange" | "Notification" | "PermissionRequest" | "PostCompact" | "PostModel" | "PostToolUse" | "PostToolUseFailure" | "PreCompact" | "PreModel" | "PreToolUse" | "PromptSubmit" | "ResponseComplete" | "SessionEnd" | "SessionStart" | "SubagentStart" | "SubagentStop" | "ApprovalExpired" | "ApprovalGranted" | "ApprovalRequested" | "MemoryDecisionStored" | "MemoryLearningStored" | "MemoryObserved" | "MemoryPatternStored" | "PipelineManifestAppended" | "PipelineStageCompleted" | "SessionEnded" | "SessionStarted" | "TaskBlocked" | "TaskCompleted" | "TaskCreated" | "TaskStarted", HookMapping>

Mapping of every canonical event to this provider's native representation.

#### providerOnlyEvents

string[]

Native event names that exist only in this provider with no canonical equivalent.

### NormalizedHookEvent

NormalizedHookEvent

A fully resolved hook event with both canonical and native names.

Members:

#### canonical

"ConfigChange" | "Notification" | "PermissionRequest" | "PostCompact" | "PostModel" | "PostToolUse" | "PostToolUseFailure" | "PreCompact" | "PreModel" | "PreToolUse" | "PromptSubmit" | "ResponseComplete" | "SessionEnd" | "SessionStart" | "SubagentStart" | "SubagentStop" | "ApprovalExpired" | "ApprovalGranted" | "ApprovalRequested" | "MemoryDecisionStored" | "MemoryLearningStored" | "MemoryObserved" | "MemoryPatternStored" | "PipelineManifestAppended" | "PipelineStageCompleted" | "SessionEnded" | "SessionStarted" | "TaskBlocked" | "TaskCompleted" | "TaskCreated" | "TaskStarted"

The CAAMP canonical event name.

#### native

string

The provider-native event name.

#### providerId

string

The provider this event was resolved for.

#### category

"agent" | "context" | "memory" | "pipeline" | "prompt" | "session" | "task" | "tool"

The lifecycle category of this event.

#### canBlock

boolean

Whether a handler for this event can block the associated action.

### HookSupportResult

HookSupportResult

Result of querying whether a provider supports a specific canonical event.

Members:

#### canonical

"ConfigChange" | "Notification" | "PermissionRequest" | "PostCompact" | "PostModel" | "PostToolUse" | "PostToolUseFailure" | "PreCompact" | "PreModel" | "PreToolUse" | "PromptSubmit" | "ResponseComplete" | "SessionEnd" | "SessionStart" | "SubagentStart" | "SubagentStop" | "ApprovalExpired" | "ApprovalGranted" | "ApprovalRequested" | "MemoryDecisionStored" | "MemoryLearningStored" | "MemoryObserved" | "MemoryPatternStored" | "PipelineManifestAppended" | "PipelineStageCompleted" | "SessionEnded" | "SessionStarted" | "TaskBlocked" | "TaskCompleted" | "TaskCreated" | "TaskStarted"

The canonical event that was queried.

#### supported

boolean

Whether the provider supports this event.

#### native

string | null

The provider-native event name, or `null` if unsupported.

#### notes

string | undefined

Optional notes about support caveats.

### ProviderHookSummary

ProviderHookSummary

Aggregated hook support summary for a single provider.

Members:

#### providerId

string

The provider identifier.

#### hookSystem

HookSystemType

The type of hook system the provider uses.

#### experimental

boolean

Whether the provider's hook system is experimental.

#### supportedCount

number

Number of canonical events this provider supports.

#### totalCanonical

number

Total number of canonical events in the taxonomy.

#### supported

("ConfigChange" | "Notification" | "PermissionRequest" | "PostCompact" | "PostModel" | "PostToolUse" | "PostToolUseFailure" | "PreCompact" | "PreModel" | "PreToolUse" | "PromptSubmit" | "ResponseComplete" | "SessionEnd" | "SessionStart" | "SubagentStart" | "SubagentStop" | "ApprovalExpired" | "ApprovalGranted" | "ApprovalRequested" | "MemoryDecisionStored" | "MemoryLearningStored" | "MemoryObserved" | "MemoryPatternStored" | "PipelineManifestAppended" | "PipelineStageCompleted" | "SessionEnded" | "SessionStarted" | "TaskBlocked" | "TaskCompleted" | "TaskCreated" | "TaskStarted")[]

List of canonical events this provider supports.

#### unsupported

("ConfigChange" | "Notification" | "PermissionRequest" | "PostCompact" | "PostModel" | "PostToolUse" | "PostToolUseFailure" | "PreCompact" | "PreModel" | "PreToolUse" | "PromptSubmit" | "ResponseComplete" | "SessionEnd" | "SessionStart" | "SubagentStart" | "SubagentStop" | "ApprovalExpired" | "ApprovalGranted" | "ApprovalRequested" | "MemoryDecisionStored" | "MemoryLearningStored" | "MemoryObserved" | "MemoryPatternStored" | "PipelineManifestAppended" | "PipelineStageCompleted" | "SessionEnded" | "SessionStarted" | "TaskBlocked" | "TaskCompleted" | "TaskCreated" | "TaskStarted")[]

List of canonical events this provider does not support.

#### providerOnly

string[]

Native events unique to this provider with no canonical mapping.

#### coverage

number

Percentage of canonical events supported (0-100).

### CrossProviderMatrix

CrossProviderMatrix

Cross-provider hook support matrix comparing multiple providers.

Members:

#### events

("ConfigChange" | "Notification" | "PermissionRequest" | "PostCompact" | "PostModel" | "PostToolUse" | "PostToolUseFailure" | "PreCompact" | "PreModel" | "PreToolUse" | "PromptSubmit" | "ResponseComplete" | "SessionEnd" | "SessionStart" | "SubagentStart" | "SubagentStop" | "ApprovalExpired" | "ApprovalGranted" | "ApprovalRequested" | "MemoryDecisionStored" | "MemoryLearningStored" | "MemoryObserved" | "MemoryPatternStored" | "PipelineManifestAppended" | "PipelineStageCompleted" | "SessionEnded" | "SessionStarted" | "TaskBlocked" | "TaskCompleted" | "TaskCreated" | "TaskStarted")[]

The canonical events included in this matrix (rows).

#### providers

string[]

The provider IDs included in this matrix (columns).

#### matrix

Record<"ConfigChange" | "Notification" | "PermissionRequest" | "PostCompact" | "PostModel" | "PostToolUse" | "PostToolUseFailure" | "PreCompact" | "PreModel" | "PreToolUse" | "PromptSubmit" | "ResponseComplete" | "SessionEnd" | "SessionStart" | "SubagentStart" | "SubagentStop" | "ApprovalExpired" | "ApprovalGranted" | "ApprovalRequested" | "MemoryDecisionStored" | "MemoryLearningStored" | "MemoryObserved" | "MemoryPatternStored" | "PipelineManifestAppended" | "PipelineStageCompleted" | "SessionEnded" | "SessionStarted" | "TaskBlocked" | "TaskCompleted" | "TaskCreated" | "TaskStarted", Record<string, HookMapping>>

Nested record mapping each canonical event to each provider's hook mapping.

### HookMappingsFile

HookMappingsFile

Schema for the `providers/hook-mappings.json` data file.

Members:

#### version

string

Semver version string of the hook mappings schema.

#### lastUpdated

string

ISO 8601 date string of the last update to mappings data.

#### description

string

Human-readable description of the mappings file purpose.

#### canonicalEvents

Record<"ConfigChange" | "Notification" | "PermissionRequest" | "PostCompact" | "PostModel" | "PostToolUse" | "PostToolUseFailure" | "PreCompact" | "PreModel" | "PreToolUse" | "PromptSubmit" | "ResponseComplete" | "SessionEnd" | "SessionStart" | "SubagentStart" | "SubagentStop" | "ApprovalExpired" | "ApprovalGranted" | "ApprovalRequested" | "MemoryDecisionStored" | "MemoryLearningStored" | "MemoryObserved" | "MemoryPatternStored" | "PipelineManifestAppended" | "PipelineStageCompleted" | "SessionEnded" | "SessionStarted" | "TaskBlocked" | "TaskCompleted" | "TaskCreated" | "TaskStarted", CanonicalEventDefinition>

Definitions for every canonical event in the taxonomy.

#### providerMappings

Record<string, ProviderHookProfile>

Hook profiles keyed by provider ID.

### MarketplaceAdapter

MarketplaceAdapter

Contract that each marketplace backend adapter must implement.

Members:

#### name

string

Human-readable adapter name (e.g. `"agentskills.in"`).

#### search()

(query: string, limit?: number) => Promise<MarketplaceResult[]>

Search the marketplace for skills matching a query.

#### getSkill()

(scopedName: string) => Promise<MarketplaceResult | null>

Retrieve a single skill by its scoped name.

### MarketplaceResult

MarketplaceResult

Normalized marketplace record returned by all adapters.

Members:

#### name

string

Short skill name (e.g. `"memory"`).

#### scopedName

string

Scoped name including author prefix (e.g. `"\@anthropic/memory"`).

#### description

string

Short description of what the skill does.

#### author

string

Author or organization name.

#### stars

number

GitHub star count.

#### githubUrl

string

Full GitHub repository URL.

#### repoFullName

string

GitHub `owner/repo` path.

#### path

string

Path within the repository to the skill file.

#### source

string

Name of the marketplace source this result came from.

### SearchOptions

SearchOptions

Options for marketplace search requests.

Members:

#### query

string

Free-text search query.

#### limit

number | undefined

Maximum number of results.

#### offset

number | undefined

Pagination offset.

#### sortBy

"stars" | "recent" | "name" | undefined

Sort order for results.

#### category

string | undefined

Filter by skill category.

#### author

string | undefined

Filter by author name.

### RecommendationValidationIssue

RecommendationValidationIssue

Describes a single validation issue found in recommendation criteria.

Members:

#### code

RecommendationErrorCode

The error code identifying the type of validation failure.

#### field

"query" | "mustHave" | "prefer" | "exclude"

The criteria field that caused the validation issue.

#### message

string

A human-readable description of the validation issue.

### RecommendationValidationResult

RecommendationValidationResult

Result of validating recommendation criteria input.

Members:

#### valid

boolean

Whether the criteria passed all validation checks.

#### issues

RecommendationValidationIssue[]

List of validation issues found, empty when valid.

### RecommendationCriteriaInput

RecommendationCriteriaInput

Raw user-provided criteria for skill recommendations.

Members:

#### query

string | undefined

Free-text search query to match against skill metadata.

#### mustHave

string | string[] | undefined

Terms that a skill must match to be considered relevant.

#### prefer

string | string[] | undefined

Terms that boost a skill's score when matched.

#### exclude

string | string[] | undefined

Terms that penalize a skill's score when matched.

### NormalizedRecommendationCriteria

NormalizedRecommendationCriteria

Normalized and tokenized form of recommendation criteria.

Members:

#### query

string

The lowercased, trimmed query string.

#### queryTokens

string[]

Individual tokens extracted from the query string.

#### mustHave

string[]

Sorted, deduplicated list of required match terms.

#### prefer

string[]

Sorted, deduplicated list of preferred match terms.

#### exclude

string[]

Sorted, deduplicated list of exclusion terms.

### RecommendationReason

RecommendationReason

A single reason contributing to a skill's recommendation score.

Members:

#### code

RecommendationReasonCode

The reason code identifying the scoring signal.

#### detail

string | undefined

Optional detail providing additional context, such as match count.

### RecommendationScoreBreakdown

RecommendationScoreBreakdown

Detailed breakdown of a skill's recommendation score by category.

Members:

#### mustHave

number

Score contribution from must-have term matches.

#### prefer

number

Score contribution from preferred term matches.

#### query

number

Score contribution from query token matches.

#### stars

number

Score contribution from repository star count signal.

#### metadata

number

Score contribution from metadata quality and source confidence.

#### modernity

number

Score contribution from modern vs legacy marker detection.

#### exclusionPenalty

number

Penalty applied for matching excluded terms.

#### total

number

The final aggregated recommendation score.

### RankedSkillRecommendation

RankedSkillRecommendation

A single skill recommendation with its computed score and explanations.

Members:

#### skill

MarketplaceResult

The marketplace skill result being scored.

#### score

number

The computed recommendation score, higher is better.

#### reasons

RecommendationReason[]

List of reasons explaining the score contributions.

#### tradeoffs

string[]

Human-readable tradeoff warnings for the skill.

#### excluded

boolean

Whether the skill matched one or more exclusion terms.

#### breakdown

RecommendationScoreBreakdown | undefined

Optional detailed score breakdown by category.

### RecommendationOptions

RecommendationOptions

Configuration options for the skill recommendation engine.

Members:

#### top

number | undefined

Maximum number of results to return from the ranked list.

#### includeDetails

boolean | undefined

Whether to include detailed score breakdown in each result.

#### weights

Partial<RecommendationWeights> | undefined

Partial weight overrides for individual scoring factors.

#### modernMarkers

string[] | undefined

Custom modern technology marker strings for modernity scoring.

#### legacyMarkers

string[] | undefined

Custom legacy technology marker strings for modernity scoring.

### RecommendationWeights

RecommendationWeights

Numeric weights controlling the recommendation scoring algorithm.

Members:

#### mustHaveMatch

number

Weight applied per must-have term match.

#### preferMatch

number

Weight applied per preferred term match.

#### queryTokenMatch

number

Weight applied per query token match.

#### starsFactor

number

Multiplier for the logarithmic star count signal.

#### metadataBoost

number

Boost for metadata quality and source confidence.

#### modernMarkerBoost

number

Boost applied per modern technology marker match.

#### legacyMarkerPenalty

number

Penalty applied per legacy technology marker match.

#### excludePenalty

number

Penalty applied per excluded term match.

#### missingMustHavePenalty

number

Penalty applied per missing must-have term.

### RecommendSkillsResult

RecommendSkillsResult

The complete result of a skill recommendation operation.

Members:

#### criteria

NormalizedRecommendationCriteria

The normalized criteria used for scoring.

#### ranking

RankedSkillRecommendation[]

Skills ranked by recommendation score, highest first.

### SearchSkillsOptions

SearchSkillsOptions

Options for searching skills via marketplace APIs.

Members:

#### limit

number | undefined

Maximum number of results to return.

### RecommendSkillsQueryOptions

RecommendSkillsQueryOptions

Options for the recommendation query combining ranking options with a result limit.

Members:

#### limit

number | undefined

Maximum number of results to return.

### ValidationIssue

ValidationIssue

A single validation issue found during SKILL.md validation.

Example:
  const issue: ValidationIssue = {
    level: "error",
    field: "name",
    message: "Missing required field: name",
  };

Members:

#### level

"error" | "warning"

Severity: `"error"` causes validation failure, `"warning"` does not.

#### field

string

The field or section that triggered the issue.

#### message

string

Human-readable description of the issue.

### ValidationResult

ValidationResult

Result of validating a SKILL.md file against the Agent Skills standard.

Example:
  const result = await validateSkill("/path/to/SKILL.md");
  if (!result.valid) {
    for (const issue of result.issues) {
      console.log(`[${issue.level}] ${issue.field}: ${issue.message}`);
    }
  }

Members:

#### valid

boolean

Whether the skill passed validation (no error-level issues).

#### issues

ValidationIssue[]

All issues found during validation.

#### metadata

Record<string, unknown> | null

Parsed frontmatter metadata, or `null` if parsing failed.

### SpawnOptions

SpawnOptions

Options for spawning a subagent.

Members:

#### prompt

string

The prompt or instruction to give the spawned agent.

#### model

string | undefined

Model to use for the spawned agent.

#### tools

string[] | undefined

Tools to make available to the spawned agent.

#### timeout

number | undefined

Timeout in milliseconds for the spawned agent.

#### isolate

boolean | undefined

Whether to isolate the spawned agent (e.g. in a worktree).

### SpawnResult

SpawnResult

Result from a spawn operation.

Members:

#### instanceId

string

Unique identifier for the spawned agent instance.

#### status

"running" | "completed" | "failed"

Current status of the spawned agent.

#### output

string | undefined

Output produced by the spawned agent.

### SpawnAdapter

SpawnAdapter

Provider-neutral interface for spawning and managing subagents.  Concrete implementations will be provider-specific (e.g. ClaudeCodeSpawnAdapter, CodexSpawnAdapter) and registered by CLEO's orchestration layer.

Members:

#### canSpawn()

(provider: Provider) => boolean

Check if a provider supports spawning via this adapter.

#### spawn()

(provider: Provider, options: SpawnOptions) => Promise<SpawnResult>

Spawn a new subagent for the given provider.

#### listRunning()

(provider: Provider) => Promise<SpawnResult[]>

List currently running subagent instances.

#### terminate()

(provider: Provider, instanceId: string) => Promise<void>

Terminate a running subagent instance.

### SkillIntegrityResult

SkillIntegrityResult

Result of checking a single skill's integrity.

Members:

#### name

string

Skill name.

#### status

SkillIntegrityStatus

Overall integrity status.

#### canonicalExists

boolean

Whether the canonical directory exists.

#### canonicalPath

string | null

Expected canonical path from lock file.

#### linkStatuses

{ providerId: string; linkPath: string; exists: boolean; isSymlink: boolean; pointsToCanonical: boolean; }[]

Provider link statuses — which agents have valid symlinks.

#### isCaampOwned

boolean

Whether this is a CAAMP-reserved (ct-*) skill.

#### issue

string | undefined

Human-readable issue description, if any.

### ResolvedImports

ResolvedImports

Result of resolving `@import` lines in content.

Members:

#### resolvedContent

string

Content with `@import` lines replaced by their resolved markdown.

#### importedFiles

string[]

Absolute paths of successfully resolved .cant files.

#### errors

string[]

Error messages for failed resolutions.

### WellKnownSkill

WellKnownSkill

A skill entry discovered via the RFC 8615 well-known endpoint.

Members:

#### name

string

Skill name.

#### description

string

Human-readable description of the skill.

#### url

string

URL where the skill content can be fetched.

## Types

### ProviderPriority

ProviderPriority

Priority tier identifier stored in registry.json.

### ProviderStatus

ProviderStatus

Lifecycle status identifier stored in registry.json.

### McpConfigFormat

McpConfigFormat

Supported MCP config file formats.

### McpTransportType

McpTransportType

MCP transport protocols a provider may advertise.

### RegistryHarnessKind

RegistryHarnessKind

Harness role category for a primary or standalone harness.

### SkillsPrecedence

SkillsPrecedence

How a provider resolves skill file precedence between vendor and agents directories.

### HookEvent

string

DEPRECATED: Use `CanonicalHookEvent` from `../hooks/types.js` for the normalized CAAMP taxonomy. This type remains for backward compatibility with registry.json's `capabilities.hooks.supported` string arrays.

Hook lifecycle event identifier from registry.json.

### RegistryHookFormat

RegistryHookFormat

The on-disk layout of a provider's hook configuration.

### RegistryHookCatalog

RegistryHookCatalog

Which native event catalog a provider's hook system uses.

### SpawnMechanism

SpawnMechanism

Mechanism a provider uses to spawn subagents.

### CtSkillEntry

SkillLibraryEntry

DEPRECATED: Use `SkillLibraryEntry` instead.

Backward-compatible alias for `SkillLibraryEntry`.

### CtValidationResult

SkillLibraryValidationResult

DEPRECATED: Use `SkillLibraryValidationResult` instead.

Backward-compatible alias for `SkillLibraryValidationResult`.

### CtValidationIssue

SkillLibraryValidationIssue

DEPRECATED: Use `SkillLibraryValidationIssue` instead.

Backward-compatible alias for `SkillLibraryValidationIssue`.

### CtProfileDefinition

SkillLibraryProfile

DEPRECATED: Use `SkillLibraryProfile` instead.

Backward-compatible alias for `SkillLibraryProfile`.

### CtDispatchMatrix

SkillLibraryDispatchMatrix

DEPRECATED: Use `SkillLibraryDispatchMatrix` instead.

Backward-compatible alias for `SkillLibraryDispatchMatrix`.

### CtManifest

SkillLibraryManifest

DEPRECATED: Use `SkillLibraryManifest` instead.

Backward-compatible alias for `SkillLibraryManifest`.

### CtManifestSkill

SkillLibraryManifestSkill

DEPRECATED: Use `SkillLibraryManifestSkill` instead.

Backward-compatible alias for `SkillLibraryManifestSkill`.

### ConfigFormat

ConfigFormat

Supported configuration file formats.  - `"json"` - Standard JSON - `"jsonc"` - JSON with comments (comment-preserving via jsonc-parser) - `"yaml"` - YAML (via js-yaml) - `"toml"` - TOML (via iarna/toml)

Example:
  const format: ConfigFormat = "jsonc";

### TransportType

TransportType

MCP server transport protocol type.  - `"stdio"` - Standard input/output (local process) - `"sse"` - Server-Sent Events (remote) - `"http"` - HTTP/Streamable HTTP (remote) - `"websocket"` - WebSocket full-duplex (remote)

Example:
  const transport: TransportType = "stdio";

### DetectionMethod

DetectionMethod

Method used to detect whether an AI agent is installed on the system.  - `"binary"` - Check if a CLI binary exists on PATH - `"directory"` - Check if known config/data directories exist - `"appBundle"` - Check for macOS .app bundle in standard app directories - `"flatpak"` - Check for Flatpak installation on Linux

### SourceType

SourceType

Classified type of an MCP server or skill source.  - `"remote"` - HTTP/HTTPS URL to a remote MCP server - `"package"` - npm package name - `"command"` - Shell command string - `"github"` - GitHub repository (URL or shorthand) - `"gitlab"` - GitLab repository URL - `"local"` - Local filesystem path - `"library"` - Built-in skill library reference

### AuditSeverity

AuditSeverity

Severity level for a security audit finding.  Ordered from most to least severe: `"critical"`  `"high"`  `"medium"`  `"low"`  `"info"`.

### InjectionStatus

InjectionStatus

Status of a CAAMP injection block in an instruction file.  - `"current"` - Injection block exists and matches expected content - `"outdated"` - Injection block exists but content differs - `"missing"` - Instruction file does not exist - `"none"` - File exists but has no CAAMP injection block

### PathScope

PathScope

Scope for path resolution, either global (user home) or project-local.

### HarnessTier

HarnessTier

Three-tier scope identifier for Pi harness operations.

### HarnessAssetKind

HarnessAssetKind

Asset kinds managed by the three-tier scope model.

### HarnessScope

HarnessScope

Scope at which a harness operation should be performed.

### ExclusivityMode

ExclusivityMode

Controls how `resolveDefaultTargetProviders` (defined in `./index.ts`) selects target providers at runtime invocation time.

### MVILevel

MVILevel

LAFS MVI disclosure level - defined locally to avoid CI module resolution issues with re-exported types.

### LAFSErrorShape

LAFSError

LAFS Error structure - re-exported from protocol as LAFSErrorShape for CAAMP compatibility.

### LAFSWarning

Warning

LAFS Warning structure - re-exported from protocol.

### McpScope

McpScope

Scope identifier for MCP config file resolution.

### McpServerEntriesByProvider

McpServerEntriesByProvider

Map of provider id → MCP server entries for that provider.

### PiCantRemoveOptions

PiCommandBaseOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi cant remove`.

### PiExtensionsRemoveOptions

PiCommandBaseOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi extensions remove`.

### PiPromptsListOptions

PiCommandBaseOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi prompts list`.

### PiPromptsRemoveOptions

PiCommandBaseOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi prompts remove`.

### PiThemesListOptions

PiCommandBaseOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi themes list`.

### PiThemesRemoveOptions

PiCommandBaseOptions

Options accepted by `caamp pi themes remove`.

### HookCategory

"agent" | "context" | "memory" | "pipeline" | "prompt" | "session" | "task" | "tool"

Union type of valid hook category strings.

### EventSource

"domain" | "provider"

Union type of valid event source types.

### CanonicalHookEvent

"ConfigChange" | "Notification" | "PermissionRequest" | "PostCompact" | "PostModel" | "PostToolUse" | "PostToolUseFailure" | "PreCompact" | "PreModel" | "PreToolUse" | "PromptSubmit" | "ResponseComplete" | "SessionEnd" | "SessionStart" | "SubagentStart" | "SubagentStop" | "ApprovalExpired" | "ApprovalGranted" | "ApprovalRequested" | "MemoryDecisionStored" | "MemoryLearningStored" | "MemoryObserved" | "MemoryPatternStored" | "PipelineManifestAppended" | "PipelineStageCompleted" | "SessionEnded" | "SessionStarted" | "TaskBlocked" | "TaskCompleted" | "TaskCreated" | "TaskStarted"

Union type of all canonical hook event names.

### HookSystemType

HookSystemType

The type of hook system a provider uses.

### HookHandlerType

HookHandlerType

The mechanism a provider uses to execute hook handlers.

### RecommendationErrorCode

RecommendationErrorCode

Union type of all recommendation error code string literals.

### RecommendationReasonCode

RecommendationReasonCode

String literal union of all reason codes emitted during skill scoring.

### SkillIntegrityStatus

SkillIntegrityStatus

Status of a single skill's integrity check.

## Variables

### AGENTS_HOME

string

Global `.agents/` home directory (`~/.agents/` or `$AGENTS_HOME`).

### LOCK_FILE_PATH

string

CAAMP lock file path (`~/.agents/.caamp-lock.json`).

### CANONICAL_SKILLS_DIR

string

Canonical skills directory (`~/.agents/skills/`).

### AGENTS_MCP_DIR

string

Global MCP directory (`~/.agents/mcp/`).

### AGENTS_MCP_SERVERS_PATH

string

Global MCP servers.json path (`~/.agents/mcp/servers.json`).

### AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH

string

Global agents config.toml path (`~/.agents/config.toml`).

### TIER_PRECEDENCE

readonly HarnessTier[]

Precedence-ordered iteration of tiers for read operations.

### DEFAULT_EXCLUSIVITY_MODE

ExclusivityMode

Default exclusivity mode used when no override is configured.

### EXCLUSIVITY_MODE_ENV_VAR

"CAAMP_EXCLUSIVITY_MODE"

Environment variable name read by `getExclusivityMode` when no programmatic override is active.

### ErrorCategories

{ readonly VALIDATION: LAFSErrorCategory; readonly AUTH: LAFSErrorCategory; readonly PERMISSION: LAFSErrorCategory; readonly NOT_FOUND: LAFSErrorCategory; readonly CONFLICT: LAFSErrorCategory; readonly RATE_LIMIT: LAFSErrorCategory; readonly TRANSIENT: LAFSErrorCategory; readonly INTERNAL: LAFSErrorCategory; readonly CONTRACT: LAFSErrorCategory; readonly MIGRATION: LAFSErrorCategory; }

Common error categories mapping for convenience.

### ErrorCodes

{ readonly FORMAT_CONFLICT: "E_FORMAT_CONFLICT"; readonly INVALID_JSON: "E_INVALID_JSON"; readonly SKILL_NOT_FOUND: "E_SKILL_NOT_FOUND"; readonly PROVIDER_NOT_FOUND: "E_PROVIDER_NOT_FOUND"; readonly MCP_SERVER_NOT_FOUND: "E_MCP_SERVER_NOT_FOUND"; readonly FILE_NOT_FOUND: "E_FILE_NOT_FOUND"; readonly INVALID_INPUT: "E_INVALID_INPUT"; readonly INVALID_CONSTRAINT: "E_INVALID_CONSTRAINT"; readonly INVALID_FORMAT: "E_INVALID_FORMAT"; readonly INSTALL_FAILED: "E_INSTALL_FAILED"; readonly REMOVE_FAILED: "E_REMOVE_FAILED"; readonly UPDATE_FAILED: "E_UPDATE_FAILED"; readonly VALIDATION_FAILED: "E_VALIDATION_FAILED"; readonly AUDIT_FAILED: "E_AUDIT_FAILED"; readonly NETWORK_ERROR: "E_NETWORK_ERROR"; readonly FILE_SYSTEM_ERROR: "E_FILE_SYSTEM_ERROR"; readonly PERMISSION_DENIED: "E_PERMISSION_DENIED"; readonly INTERNAL_ERROR: "E_INTERNAL_ERROR"; }

Common error codes for consistency.

### MCP_ERROR_CODES

{ readonly VALIDATION: "E_VALIDATION_SCHEMA"; readonly NOT_FOUND: "E_NOT_FOUND_RESOURCE"; readonly CONFLICT: "E_CONFLICT_VERSION"; readonly TRANSIENT: "E_TRANSIENT_UPSTREAM"; }

Canonical LAFS error codes used by the `caamp mcp` command group.

### DEFAULT_FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS

10000

Default timeout in milliseconds for outbound HTTP requests.

### PI_ERROR_CODES

{ readonly VALIDATION: "E_VALIDATION_SCHEMA"; readonly NOT_FOUND: "E_NOT_FOUND_RESOURCE"; readonly CONFLICT: "E_CONFLICT_VERSION"; readonly TRANSIENT: "E_TRANSIENT_UPSTREAM"; }

Canonical LAFS error codes used by the `caamp pi` command group.

### HOOK_CATEGORIES

readonly ["agent", "context", "memory", "pipeline", "prompt", "session", "task", "tool"]

All hook event categories (8 total).

### EVENT_SOURCES

readonly ["domain", "provider"]

Event source types: domain, provider.

### CANONICAL_HOOK_EVENTS

readonly ["ConfigChange", "Notification", "PermissionRequest", "PostCompact", "PostModel", "PostToolUse", "PostToolUseFailure", "PreCompact", "PreModel", "PreToolUse", "PromptSubmit", "ResponseComplete", "SessionEnd", "SessionStart", "SubagentStart", "SubagentStop", "ApprovalExpired", "ApprovalGranted", "ApprovalRequested", "MemoryDecisionStored", "MemoryLearningStored", "MemoryObserved", "MemoryPatternStored", "PipelineManifestAppended", "PipelineStageCompleted", "SessionEnded", "SessionStarted", "TaskBlocked", "TaskCompleted", "TaskCreated", "TaskStarted"]

All canonical hook events (31 total: 16 provider, 15 domain).  This replaces the previously hardcoded tuple in types.ts. Single source of truth: providers/hook-mappings.json

### PROVIDER_HOOK_EVENTS

readonly ["ConfigChange", "Notification", "PermissionRequest", "PostCompact", "PostModel", "PostToolUse", "PostToolUseFailure", "PreCompact", "PreModel", "PreToolUse", "PromptSubmit", "ResponseComplete", "SessionEnd", "SessionStart", "SubagentStart", "SubagentStop"]

Provider-sourced events only (original 16 CAAMP events).

### DOMAIN_HOOK_EVENTS

readonly ["ApprovalExpired", "ApprovalGranted", "ApprovalRequested", "MemoryDecisionStored", "MemoryLearningStored", "MemoryObserved", "MemoryPatternStored", "PipelineManifestAppended", "PipelineStageCompleted", "SessionEnded", "SessionStarted", "TaskBlocked", "TaskCompleted", "TaskCreated", "TaskStarted"]

Domain-sourced events only (CLEO business events).

### EVENT_METADATA

Record<"ConfigChange" | "Notification" | "PermissionRequest" | "PostCompact" | "PostModel" | "PostToolUse" | "PostToolUseFailure" | "PreCompact" | "PreModel" | "PreToolUse" | "PromptSubmit" | "ResponseComplete" | "SessionEnd" | "SessionStart" | "SubagentStart" | "SubagentStop" | "ApprovalExpired" | "ApprovalGranted" | "ApprovalRequested" | "MemoryDecisionStored" | "MemoryLearningStored" | "MemoryObserved" | "MemoryPatternStored" | "PipelineManifestAppended" | "PipelineStageCompleted" | "SessionEnded" | "SessionStarted" | "TaskBlocked" | "TaskCompleted" | "TaskCreated" | "TaskStarted", { category: HookCategory; source: EventSource; canBlock: boolean; description: string; }>

Metadata for each canonical event, derived from hook-mappings.json.

### AUDIT_RULES

AuditRule[]

Complete set of security audit rules for SKILL.md scanning.

### RECOMMENDATION_ERROR_CODES

{ readonly QUERY_INVALID: "E_SKILLS_QUERY_INVALID"; readonly NO_MATCHES: "E_SKILLS_NO_MATCHES"; readonly SOURCE_UNAVAILABLE: "E_SKILLS_SOURCE_UNAVAILABLE"; readonly CRITERIA_CONFLICT: "E_SKILLS_CRITERIA_CONFLICT"; }

Error codes used in skill recommendation validation.

### rankSkills

(skills: MarketplaceResult[], criteriaInput: RecommendationCriteriaInput, options?: RecommendationOptions) => RecommendSkillsResult

Alias for `recommendSkills` providing a shorter function name.
